questions.explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar chaired the Drafting Committee of the Indian Constitution.

questions.text: The Magna Carta was signed in 1215 and limited the powers of the English King.
questions.explanation: The Magna Carta was indeed signed in 1215 by King John.

questions.text: Which article of the Indian Constitution grants fundamental rights?
questions.explanation: Articles 12-35 cover Fundamental Rights in the Indian Constitution.

questions.text: Match the independence leaders with their independence movements:
questions.explanation: These are major independence and liberation leaders.

questions.text: The Sepoy Mutiny occurred in which year?
questions.explanation: The Sepoy Mutiny began in 1857 and lasted until 1859.

questions.text: The French Revolution took place in the 18th century.
questions.explanation: The French Revolution occurred in 1789-1799 period.

questions.text: Which of these were Mughal emperors? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Akbar, Shah Jahan, and Aurangzeb were Mughal emperors. Ashoka and Vikramaditya were different dynasty rulers.

questions.text: Assertion: Democracy requires a constitution. Reason: A constitution defines governmental structure.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct and logically connected.

questions.text: The Universal Declaration of Human Rights was adopted in which year?
questions.explanation: The UDHR was adopted by the UN General Assembly on December 10, 1948.

questions.text: The Indian Constitution has ______ Articles and ______ Schedules.
questions.explanation: The Constitution originally had 395 articles (now 470+ after amendments) and 12 schedules.
